ogTitle: Pleasant Hills vs Plumpton: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Pleasant Hills and Plumpton.
heading: Pleasant Hills vs Plumpton — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Pleasant Hills, New South Wales and Plumpton, New South Wales. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Plumpton has a lower overall climate risk score (38/100) compared to Pleasant Hills (44/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Pleasant Hills or Plumpton safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Pleasant Hills has a flood risk score of 59/100, while Plumpton scores 40/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Pleasant Hills scores 59/100 for bushfire risk. Plumpton scores 50/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Pleasant Hills or Plumpton?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Plumpton has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 2.05% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
